moments.title: The First World Cup Match
moments.subtitle: France vs Mexico
moments.description: The very first FIFA World Cup match kicked off in Montevideo, Uruguay on July 13, 1930. France defeated Mexico 4–1, with Lucien Laurent etching his name into football history forever.

moments.title: The First World Cup Goal
moments.subtitle: Lucien Laurent · France
moments.description: In the 19th minute of football's first World Cup match, Frenchman Lucien Laurent struck the ball into the net to score the first goal in World Cup history. He was 22 years old.

moments.title: Uruguay Wins the First World Cup
moments.subtitle: Uruguay vs Argentina
moments.description: Before 93,000 fans in Montevideo's Estadio Centenario, Uruguay came from 2–1 down at half-time to beat Argentina 4–2 and become the first FIFA World Cup champions.

moments.title: USA Stuns England
moments.subtitle: USA 1–0 England
moments.description: In one of the greatest upsets in World Cup history, amateur Americans defeated the inventors of football 1–0. Joe Gaetjens headed the winner in the 37th minute. England were among the tournament favourites.

moments.title: The Maracanazo
moments.subtitle: Uruguay 2–1 Brazil
moments.description: With 200,000 fans expecting a Brazilian coronation, Uruguay produced the greatest upset in football history. Ghiggia's winner silenced the Maracanã and traumatised a nation. The crowd stood in stunned silence.

moments.title: The Miracle of Bern
moments.subtitle: West Germany 3–2 Hungary
moments.description: Hungary were unbeaten in four years and had thrashed West Germany 8–3 in the group stage. But Helmut Rahn's winner in the 84th minute gave West Germany the title and became a symbol of post-war national rebirth.

moments.title: The Greatest Goalfest
moments.subtitle: Austria 7–5 Switzerland
moments.description: Twelve goals in a World Cup knockout match. Austria trailed 3–0 before half-time then scored seven unanswered goals. It remains the highest-scoring match in World Cup history.

moments.title: Pelé Makes His World Cup Debut
moments.subtitle: Brazil vs USSR
moments.description: A 17-year-old from Bauru, São Paulo stepped onto the World Cup stage for the first time. Pelé's extraordinary talent was immediately visible and the football world took notice of the youngest player at the tournament.

moments.title: Pelé Wins the World Cup at 17
moments.subtitle: Brazil 5–2 Sweden
moments.description: Scoring twice in the final — including a stunning chest-control-and-volley — 17-year-old Pelé became the youngest World Cup winner in history. He wept on the shoulder of goalkeeper Gilmar at the final whistle.

moments.title: North Korea Stuns Italy
moments.subtitle: North Korea 1–0 Italy
moments.description: In the biggest shock of the era, North Korea eliminated three-time World Cup finalists Italy. Pak Doo-ik scored the only goal midway through the second half. The Italian players were reportedly pelted with rotten tomatoes upon returning home.

moments.title: England Wins Its Only World Cup
moments.subtitle: England 4–2 West Germany (AET)
moments.description: Geoff Hurst's disputed third goal — did it cross the line? — remains the most debated moment in football history. He then completed the only World Cup final hat-trick to give England their one and only world title.

moments.title: Brazil's Greatest Team Wins the World Cup
moments.subtitle: Brazil 4–1 Italy
moments.description: Pelé, Jairzinho, Tostão, Rivelino, Carlos Alberto — the greatest football team ever assembled won the 1970 World Cup with a performance so beautiful that Italy's players applauded them off the pitch.

moments.title: The Cruyff Turn
moments.subtitle: Netherlands vs Sweden
moments.description: A single feint against Swedish defender Jan Olsson became the most imitated move in football history. Johan Cruyff dragged the ball behind his standing leg and left Olsson completely bewildered, defining Total Football in one moment.

moments.title: Paolo Rossi Destroys Brazil
moments.subtitle: Italy 3–2 Brazil
moments.description: Brazil needed only a draw to advance but Paolo Rossi — recalled from a two-year ban — scored a hat-trick to eliminate the tournament favourites. Considered one of the greatest matches in World Cup history.

moments.title: Italy Wins Third World Cup
moments.subtitle: Italy 3–1 West Germany
moments.description: Paolo Rossi — who had returned from a two-year betting suspension — finished as the tournament's top scorer and propelled Italy to their third World Cup title. Enzo Bearzot's side had been booed on arrival home weeks earlier.

moments.title: The Hand of God
moments.subtitle: Argentina vs England
moments.stage: Quarter Final · Azteca Stadium, Mexico City
moments.description: Diego Maradona punched the ball into the net with his left hand in the 51st minute, then told reporters the goal was scored "a little with the head of Maradona and a little with the hand of God." The referee missed it. England were eliminated.

moments.title: Goal of the Century
moments.subtitle: Argentina vs England
moments.stage: Quarter Final · Azteca Stadium, Mexico City
moments.description: Four minutes after the Hand of God, Maradona collected the ball in his own half and dribbled past five England players and the goalkeeper before rolling the ball into an empty net. FIFA later voted it the Goal of the Century.

moments.title: Maradona Lifts the World Cup
moments.subtitle: Argentina 3–2 West Germany
moments.description: Argentina trailed 2–0 in the second half before fighting back. Jorge Burruchaga scored the winner six minutes from time. Maradona, the tournament's dominant figure, lifted the trophy to complete one of football's greatest individual performances.

moments.title: Cameroon Shocks the World Champions
moments.subtitle: Cameroon 1–0 Argentina
moments.description: In the opening match of Italia 90, defending champions Argentina were beaten by Cameroon's Indomitable Lions. 38-year-old Roger Milla came off the bench to inspire the tournament's great story. Two Cameroon players were sent off.

moments.title: Roger Milla and the Corner Flag
moments.subtitle: Cameroon vs Colombia
moments.description: 38-year-old Roger Milla came off the bench to score twice against Colombia, each time celebrating by dancing around the corner flag. The joyous celebration became one of the most iconic images in World Cup history.

moments.title: Baggio's Penalty Miss
moments.subtitle: Brazil vs Italy
moments.description: Roberto Baggio — Italy's hero throughout the tournament — stepped up to take the decisive penalty in the first World Cup final decided by a shootout. His shot sailed over the bar. Brazil won. Baggio stood motionless, head bowed, ponytail hanging.

moments.title: Brazil Wins Fourth World Cup
moments.subtitle: Brazil 0–0 Italy (3–2 pens)
moments.description: The first World Cup final to be decided by penalties. Brazil, led by Romário and Bebeto, were crowned world champions for a record fourth time. The image of Baggio's missed penalty defined the tournament.

moments.title: Zidane's Masterclass
moments.subtitle: France 3–0 Brazil
moments.description: Zinedine Zidane headed in two first-half corners to give France a stunning lead. Playing in front of their own fans, Les Bleus demolished Brazil — despite a late Ronaldo appearance amid a health scare mystery — to win their first World Cup.

moments.title: Bergkamp's Immortal Goal
moments.subtitle: Netherlands vs Argentina
moments.description: In the 89th minute with the score level, Dennis Bergkamp received a 50-yard pass, controlled it with his first touch, beat the defender with his second, and finished with his third. Considered the finest goal in World Cup history.

moments.title: Senegal Defeats the World Champions
moments.subtitle: Senegal 1–0 France
moments.description: Reigning world and European champions France were eliminated in the group stage after losing their opening match to debutants Senegal. Papa Bouba Diop scored the winner. The Senegalese players danced in celebration around the corner flag.

moments.title: Ronaldo's Redemption
moments.subtitle: Brazil 2–0 Germany
moments.description: Four years after a mysterious pre-match health scare derailed the 1998 final, Ronaldo returned to score twice against Germany to win Brazil's fifth World Cup. He finished as tournament top scorer with eight goals.

moments.title: Ronaldinho Lobs Seaman
moments.subtitle: Brazil 2–1 England
moments.description: Ronaldinho's speculative 40-yard free-kick looped over a stranded David Seaman and into the net. Whether it was intentional or a cross remains debated. England were eliminated and Ronaldinho — sent off shortly after — became a global superstar.

moments.title: Zidane's Headbutt
moments.subtitle: France vs Italy
moments.description: In the final match of his career, Zinedine Zidane headbutted Italian defender Marco Materazzi in the chest after a verbal altercation. He was sent off. Italy won on penalties. It was the most extraordinary ending to any player's career.

moments.title: Maxi Rodríguez's Golden Goal
moments.subtitle: Argentina vs Mexico
moments.description: In extra time, the ball fell to Maxi Rodríguez 25 yards from goal. He let it drop over his shoulder and volleyed it into the top corner with his left foot. It remains one of the best World Cup goals ever scored.

moments.title: Iniesta Wins It for Spain
moments.subtitle: Spain 1–0 Netherlands (AET)
moments.description: In the 116th minute of extra time, Andrés Iniesta controlled a pass on the edge of the area and struck it into the net. Spain won their first World Cup, completing an unprecedented treble of major trophies. Iniesta dedicated the goal to his recently deceased friend.

moments.title: Suárez's Handball & Ghana's Heartbreak
moments.subtitle: Ghana vs Uruguay
moments.description: In the final seconds of extra time, Luis Suárez deliberately blocked Dominic Adiyiah's goalbound header with both hands. He was sent off. Asamoah Gyan hit the penalty against the bar. Uruguay survived on penalties. Africa's World Cup dream was over.

moments.title: Van Persie's Flying Header
moments.subtitle: Netherlands vs Spain
moments.description: Daley Blind played a perfectly weighted ball over the Spanish defence. Robin van Persie launched himself horizontally and headed it into the bottom corner from outside the area while parallel to the ground. Spain, the world champions, lost 5–1.

moments.title: James Rodríguez's Perfect Volley
moments.subtitle: Colombia vs Uruguay
moments.description: James Rodríguez controlled a long ball on his chest, let it drop and struck a left-footed volley into the top corner from outside the area. It won the Puskás Award for goal of the year and made a 22-year-old a global superstar overnight.

moments.title: Germany 7–1 Brazil
moments.subtitle: Germany vs Brazil
moments.stage: Semi Final · Estádio Mineirão, Belo Horizonte
moments.description: The darkest night in Brazilian football history. In their own backyard, Brazil were demolished 7–1 by Germany. Five goals came in an astonishing 18-minute spell. The Mineirazo joined the Maracanazo in Brazilian football trauma.

moments.title: Götze's Extra-Time Winner
moments.subtitle: Germany 1–0 Argentina (AET)
moments.description: Mario Götze came off the bench in extra time and, in the 113th minute, controlled a cross on his chest and volleyed past Romero to win Germany their fourth World Cup title. Joachim Löw had told him: 'Show the world you're better than Messi.'

moments.title: Saudi Arabia Stuns Argentina
moments.subtitle: Saudi Arabia 2–1 Argentina
moments.description: Lionel Messi gave Argentina the lead from the penalty spot but Saudi Arabia, using a high defensive line to trap Argentina offside 10 times in the first half, came back to win 2–1. It was Argentina's first World Cup group stage defeat since 2002.

moments.title: Messi Finally Lifts the Trophy
moments.subtitle: Argentina 3–3 France (4–2 pens)
moments.description: After 36 years, Argentina won the World Cup again. Lionel Messi scored twice and converted a penalty in the shootout. Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick for France to force extra time. When the final whistle came, Messi sank to his knees.

moments.title: Mbappé's Final Hat-Trick
moments.subtitle: France vs Argentina
moments.description: Trailing 3–1 with 10 minutes to go, Kylian Mbappé scored twice in two minutes — a penalty and a stunning volley — to level the match and force extra time. He later completed the hat-trick in the shootout. Only the second hat-trick in a World Cup final.

moments.title: Brazil Wins Its First World Cup
moments.subtitle: Brazil 5–2 Sweden
moments.description: Brazil became world champions for the first time, defeating Sweden in their own backyard. The 17-year-old Pelé scored twice and wept openly at the final whistle, becoming the youngest World Cup winner in history.

moments.title: Carlos Alberto's Team Goal
moments.subtitle: Brazil vs Italy
moments.description: The final goal in the greatest final. A nine-pass move involving almost every outfield player culminated in captain Carlos Alberto arriving at full speed to thunderously convert. Pelé's perfectly timed no-look pass set him up perfectly.

moments.title: Total Football Meets Its Match
moments.subtitle: West Germany 2–1 Netherlands
moments.description: Johan Cruyff's Netherlands were the most exciting team in the world, but West Germany — on home soil — won the title. Netherlands took the lead before West Germany touched the ball, but ultimately Sepp Maier lifted the trophy.

moments.title: Italy Wins Fourth World Cup
moments.subtitle: Italy 1–1 France (5–3 pens)
moments.description: A final defined by Zidane's headbutt. Italy won on penalties after Fabio Grosso converted the decisive kick. For France, the abrupt end of Zidane's career on the biggest stage remained the dominant story.

moments.title: Croatia's Comeback vs England
moments.subtitle: Croatia 2–1 England (AET)
moments.description: England led through Kieran Trippier's stunning free-kick, but Ivan Perišić equalised and Mario Mandžukić won it in extra time. Croatia, a nation of 4 million people, reached their first World Cup final.

moments.title: France Wins Its Second World Cup
moments.subtitle: France 4–2 Croatia
moments.description: An entertaining final included an own goal, a controversial handball penalty, and a goalkeeping error. Kylian Mbappé became only the second teenager after Pelé to score in a World Cup final, as France claimed their second title.

moments.title: Teenage Mbappé Joins Pelé in the Record Books
moments.subtitle: France vs Croatia
moments.description: At 19 years and 207 days, Kylian Mbappé scored in the World Cup final to join Pelé as the only teenagers to achieve the feat. A new era of football had a face.

moments.title: The Most Cynical World Cup Final
moments.subtitle: West Germany 1–0 Argentina
moments.description: Regarded as the worst World Cup final in history, the match featured two red cards, multiple cynical fouls, and was decided by a controversial 85th-minute penalty. Maradona wept as his team were beaten.

moments.title: Last-Gasp Drama: USA vs Portugal
moments.subtitle: USA 2–2 Portugal
moments.description: The USA led 2–1 heading into stoppage time, seemingly on course to qualify. Then Cristiano Ronaldo delivered a pinpoint cross and Silvestre Varela headed in an equaliser in the 95th minute. American hearts were broken.

moments.title: Uruguay Reaches the Semi-Finals
moments.subtitle: Uruguay vs Ghana
moments.description: Sixty years after the Maracanazo, Uruguay were back in a World Cup semi-final — the smallest country (by population) to reach the last four since 1966. Luis Suárez's handball was the villain, but Forlán's brilliance throughout told the real story.

moments.title: The World Cup Comes to England
moments.subtitle: Opening Day · Wembley Stadium
moments.description: England hosted the World Cup for the first and only time in 1966. The tournament began at Wembley, and the host nation ultimately went all the way — lifting the Jules Rimet Trophy on home soil on July 30th.

moments.title: Brazil Hosts the World Cup
moments.subtitle: Brazil 3–1 Croatia
moments.stage: Opening Match · Arena Corinthians, São Paulo
moments.description: Brazil opened the 2014 World Cup on home soil with a 3–1 win over Croatia. Neymar scored twice, including a 71st-minute goal that sparked wild celebrations. But Oscar's 91st-minute third goal was what the packed stadium — and the world — had waited for.
